objectives: Understand and use vocabulary related to leisure activities, such as DIY, knitting kit, leisure time, hang out, cinema, comedy.
Practice the present continuous and simple present tenses in the context of talking about hobbies and leisure activities.
Develop listening and reading comprehension skills through dialogues.
Engage in pair and group activities to practice speaking and writing skills.
materials: Printed copies of the dialogue for students

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of leisure activities and engage students' interest.
stages.content: Teacher greets students and asks them about their favorite leisure activities.

activities.action: Ask students: 'What do you like to do in your free time?' and write their responses on the board.
Respond to the teacher's question with their favorite leisure activities.

stages.name: Stage 1: Listen and Read
stages.aim: To introduce the dialogue and practice listening and reading skills.
stages.target_language: I’m keen on doing DIY, knitting kit, leisure time, hang out, cinema, comedy.
stages.content: Students listen to the dialogue between Tom and Trang and follow along in their textbooks.

activities.action: Play the audio recording of the dialogue.
Listen to the recording and follow the text in their books.
Ask students to read the dialogue aloud in pairs.

stages.name: Stage 2: Complete the Sentences
stages.aim: To check comprehension of the dialogue and practice sentence completion.
stages.target_language: knitting kit, leisure time, free time, together, Sunday
stages.content: Students complete sentences based on the dialogue.

activities.action: Instruct students to complete the sentences on page 8.

Complete the sentences: 1. Trang is looking for a _________. 2. She likes doing DIY in her _________. 3. Tom loves spending his free time with _________. 4. Tom and his friends usually _________ together. 5. Tom, Mark, Trang, and Mai are going to see a comedy this _________.

stages.name: Stage 3: Match Activities with Pictures
stages.aim: To practice vocabulary related to leisure activities by matching words to pictures.
stages.target_language: doing DIY, doing puzzles, surfing the net, cooking, playing sport, messaging friends
stages.content: Students match activities from the box with pictures.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_26]
Display the images on the board and ask students to match the activities from the box with the pictures.
Work in pairs to write the activities under the correct pictures.

stages.name: Stage 4: Guess the Activities
stages.aim: To practice understanding descriptions and matching them to activities.
stages.target_language: improve memory, save money, improve physical health, keep in touch, learn something about IT
stages.content: Students read phrases and guess which activities are described.

activities.action: Read the phrases aloud and ask students to guess the activities.
Work in pairs to match the phrases with the activities from Stage 3.

stages.name: Stage 5: Group Discussion
stages.aim: To practice speaking by discussing weekend plans and reporting to the class.
stages.target_language: If you have some free time this weekend, what will you do?
stages.content: Students discuss their weekend plans in groups and report back to the class.

activities.action: Ask students to discuss the question: 'If you have some free time this weekend, what will you do?' in groups.
Discuss in groups and then report their answers to the class.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and consolidate learning.
stages.content: Teacher reviews key vocabulary and grammar points from the lesson.

activities.action: Review the key vocabulary and grammar points from the lesson.
Participate in the review by answering questions.

objectives: Understand and use expressions related to likes and dislikes such as 'be fond of', 'be keen on', 'be interested in', 'be crazy about', 'be into'.
Practice using negation in expressions of likes and dislikes by adding 'not' after the verb 'be'.
Improve pronunciation skills by distinguishing between the sounds /ʊ/ and /u:/.

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of likes and dislikes and engage students' interest.
stages.content: Teacher greets students and asks them about their favorite hobbies and interests.

activities.action: Ask students: 'What are some activities you like or dislike?' and write their responses on the board.
Respond to the teacher's question with activities they like or dislike.

stages.name: Stage 1: Vocabulary Matching
stages.aim: To match words and phrases to form expressions about likes.
stages.target_language: be fond of, be keen on, be interested in, be crazy about, be into
stages.content: Students match words from column A with column B to make expressions about likes.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_39]
Explain the task and demonstrate one example. Then ask students to complete the matching exercise.
Work individually to match the words and phrases in column A with those in column B.

stages.name: Stage 2: Negation of Likes and Dislikes
stages.aim: To practice changing positive expressions about likes into negative ones.
stages.content: Students learn how to express dislikes by adding 'not' after the verb 'be'.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_40]
Explain how to change expressions of likes to dislikes by adding 'not' after 'be'. Provide examples.
Practice changing given sentences from positive to negative.

stages.name: Stage 3: Fill in the Blanks
stages.aim: To practice using vocabulary in context by filling in blanks with correct words.
stages.content: Students fill in the blanks with the correct words from the box.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_42]
Instruct students to fill in the blanks with the correct words.

Complete the sentences: 1. I'm ________ of taking photos. 2. Mai is ________ in history. 3. My sister is ________ on cooking. 4. My brother spends lots of time surfing the net, but I'm not ________ it. 5. I'm not crazy ________ doing DIY.

stages.name: Stage 4: Sentence Completion
stages.aim: To express personal likes and dislikes using given vocabulary.
stages.target_language: crazy about, keen on, fond of, not interested in, not into
stages.content: Students complete sentences about their own likes and dislikes.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_46]
Ask students to complete the sentences with words from the box or their own ideas.
Write sentences: 1. I'm crazy about ________. 2. I'm keen on ________. 3. I'm fond of ________. 4. I'm not interested in ________. 5. I'm not into ________.

stages.name: Stage 5: Pronunciation Practice
stages.aim: To practice pronunciation of /ʊ/ and /u:/ sounds.
stages.content: Students listen and repeat words, focusing on the sounds /ʊ/ and /u:/.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_50]
Play the audio recording and ask students to repeat the words.
Listen and repeat: cook, school, group, would, push, woman, June, move. Then categorize the words into /ʊ/ and /u:/ columns.

stages.name: Stage 6: Sentence Pronunciation Practice
stages.aim: To practice pronunciation in context by identifying /ʊ/ and /u:/ sounds in sentences.
stages.content: Students listen to sentences and identify the sounds /ʊ/ and /u:/ in context.

activities.action: Play the audio of sentences and instruct students to underline /ʊ/ words and circle /u:/ words.

Listen and practice: 1. She likes reading books and swimming in the pool. 2. When it is cool, we like to play football. 3. She drew and made puddings in her free time. 4. My mum loves pumpkin soup and coffee with a little sugar. 5. My brother is fond of watching the cartoon about a clever wolf.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and consolidate learning.
stages.target_language: Expressions of likes and dislikes, /ʊ/ and /u:/ sounds
stages.content: Teacher reviews key points and addresses any questions.

activities.action: Summarize the lesson's key vocabulary and pronunciation points.
Participate in the review by asking questions or sharing what they learned.

objectives: Understand and use verbs of liking and disliking followed by gerunds and to-infinitives.
Practice distinguishing between verbs that can only be followed by gerunds and those that can be followed by both gerunds and to-infinitives.
Improve sentence completion skills using verbs of liking and disliking.
Engage in interactive activities to reinforce the use of likes and dislikes vocabulary.

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of verbs of liking and disliking and engage students' interest.
stages.content: Teacher greets students and asks them about activities they like or dislike doing.

activities.action: Ask students: 'What activities do you love or hate doing?' and write their responses on the board.
Respond to the teacher's question with activities they love or hate.

stages.name: Stage 1: Verb Categorization
stages.aim: To categorize verbs based on whether they are followed by gerunds only or both gerunds and to-infinitives.
stages.target_language: love, dislike, like, enjoy, detest, hate, fancy, prefer
stages.content: Students categorize verbs into two columns based on their usage with gerunds and to-infinitives.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_58]
Explain the difference between verbs followed by gerunds only and those followed by both gerunds and to-infinitives. Provide examples.
Work in pairs to categorize the verbs into the correct columns.

stages.name: Stage 2: Multiple Choice Exercise
stages.aim: To practice choosing the correct form of verbs in sentences.
stages.target_language: travelling, doing, to watch, going, hunting
stages.content: Students choose the correct verb form in given sentences.

activities.action: Explain the task and read the sentences aloud.

Individually choose the correct answer A, B, or C for each sentence: 1. I love ________, so in my leisure time, I go to some villages near Ha Noi to relax and enjoy the outdoors. 2. Tom enjoys ________ puzzles, especially Sudoku. 3. When do you like ________ TV? 4. Do you fancy ________ to the cinema this weekend? 5. I detest ________. I think it's cruel to harm animals.

stages.name: Stage 3: Sentence Completion with Pictures
stages.aim: To complete sentences using verbs in brackets based on pictures.
stages.content: Students complete sentences with the correct form of the verb in brackets.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_65]
Show the pictures and instruct students to complete the sentences using the verbs in brackets.
Write the sentences: 1. Mark __________ (like) 2. The girls __________ (enjoy) 3. My cousin __________ (dislike) 4. My father __________ (hate) 5. Tom and his sister __________ (prefer)

stages.name: Stage 4: Personal Sentence Completion
stages.aim: To express personal likes and dislikes using the target vocabulary.
stages.target_language: I like, I prefer, I love, I dislike, I hate, I detest
stages.content: Students complete sentences about their own preferences.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_68]
Ask students to complete the sentences about themselves.
Write sentences: I like ______. I prefer ______. I love ______. I dislike ______. I hate ______. I detest ______.

stages.name: Stage 5: Game - Likes and Dislikes Mimes
stages.aim: To practice asking and answering questions about likes and dislikes through a mime game.
stages.target_language: Do you like, Do you love, Yes, I do, No, I don't
stages.content: Students play a mime game to guess leisure activities based on yes/no questions.

activities.action: Explain the rules of the mime game and provide an example.

Work in groups. One student mimes a leisure activity they like or dislike. The others guess by asking yes/no questions using the verbs they have learned. Example: A: Do you like surfing the net? B: No, I don't. C: Do you love messaging your friends? B: Yes, I do.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and consolidate learning.
stages.target_language: Verbs of liking and disliking, gerunds, to-infinitives
stages.content: Teacher reviews key points and addresses any questions.

activities.action: Summarize the lesson's key grammar points and vocabulary.
Participate in the review by asking questions or sharing what they learned.

objectives: Understand and use phrases for inviting and accepting invitations.
Practice speaking skills through role-play and pair work.
Identify leisure activities from pictures and descriptions.
Discuss and report on personal preferences regarding leisure activities.

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of inviting and accepting invitations and engage students' interest.
stages.content: Teacher greets students and asks them about recent invitations they have received or given.

activities.action: Ask students: 'Have you invited anyone to do something recently? How did you invite them?' and write key phrases on the board.
Respond to the teacher's question with examples of invitations they have given or received.

stages.name: Stage 1: Listen and Read Dialogues
stages.aim: To listen and understand dialogues that include invitations and responses.
stages.target_language: Would you like to..., I’d love to, Do you fancy..., That’s great
stages.content: Students listen to and read the dialogues, focusing on the highlighted invitation and acceptance phrases.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_75]
Play the audio recording of the dialogues and ask students to follow along in their textbooks.
Listen to the dialogues and pay attention to the highlighted parts.
Ask students to read the dialogues aloud in pairs, focusing on pronunciation and intonation.

stages.name: Stage 2: Practise Inviting and Accepting
stages.aim: To practice inviting and accepting invitations using the target language.
stages.target_language: Would you like to..., I’d love to, Do you fancy..., That’s great
stages.content: Students practice inviting and accepting invitations in given situations.

activities.action: Explain the task and provide examples of how to invite and accept invitations.

Work in pairs to practice the dialogues in the given situations: - Invite your friend to play badminton. - Invite your friend to make paper flowers with you. - Invite your friend to try your home-made pizza.

stages.name: Stage 3: Identify Activities in Pictures
stages.aim: To identify leisure activities from pictures and discuss them.
stages.content: Students identify activities in pictures and discuss them with a partner.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_80]
Show the pictures and ask students to discuss what activity is depicted in each picture.
Work in pairs to answer the question: 'What is the activity in each picture?' and share their answers with the class.

stages.name: Stage 4: Complete the Table
stages.aim: To read descriptions of leisure activities and complete a table with information.
stages.target_language: Origami, Snowboarding, Playing sport, Relax, Health, Strength
stages.content: Students read descriptions and fill in a table with information about leisure activities.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_83]
[IMAGE_TAG_84]
Ask students to read the text about the three teenagers and complete the table with the information provided.
Fill in the table with details about each teenager's leisure activity, the country they are from, who they do the activity with, and the benefits of the activity.

stages.name: Stage 5: Group Discussion
stages.aim: To discuss personal preferences for leisure activities and report to the class.
stages.target_language: Which activity do you want to try?, Why do you want to try it?
stages.content: Students discuss in groups which activity they would like to try and why, then report their answers to the class.

activities.action: Explain the discussion task and encourage students to think about their own preferences.
Work in groups to discuss the questions: 'Which of the activities in 4 do you want to try?' and 'Why do you want to try it?' Then prepare to report their group's answers to the class.
Report their group's answers to the class, explaining their reasons for choosing the activities.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and consolidate learning.
stages.target_language: Inviting and accepting invitations, leisure activities
stages.content: Teacher reviews key phrases and vocabulary from the lesson and addresses any questions.

activities.action: Summarize the lesson's key points and ask students if they have any questions.
Participate in the review by asking questions or sharing what they learned.

objectives: Develop reading comprehension skills by identifying key information in a text about leisure activities.
Enhance speaking skills through group discussions and reporting.
Practice using the present simple tense to describe habitual actions.
Improve question formation using 'What,' 'Who,' and 'Which'.
materials: Printed copies of the text about Trang's leisure activities
Printed question sheets for group discussions

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of leisure activities and engage students' interest.
stages.content: Teacher greets students and asks them about their favorite leisure activities.

activities.action: Ask students: 'What are your favorite leisure activities?' and write their responses on the board.
Respond to the teacher's question with their favorite leisure activities.

stages.name: Stage 1: Picture Discussion
stages.aim: To identify and discuss leisure activities depicted in pictures.
stages.content: Students look at pictures and discuss the activities they see.

activities.action: Show pictures of various leisure activities and ask students to identify them.
Discuss in pairs: 'What activities can you see in the pictures?' and share their answers with the class.

stages.name: Stage 2: Reading Comprehension - Multiple Choice
stages.aim: To read about Trang's leisure activities and choose the correct answers to comprehension questions.
stages.content: Students read a text about Trang's leisure activities and answer multiple-choice questions.

activities.action: Distribute the text about Trang's leisure activities and explain the multiple-choice questions.

Read the text and choose the correct answer for each question: 1. The text is about Trang's leisure activities ______. A. in the past B. with her friends C. with her family 2. The word 'connect' is closest in meaning to ______. A. join with something B. better understand someone C. speak to someone by phone.

stages.name: Stage 3: Reading Comprehension - Answering Questions
stages.aim: To read the text again and answer detailed comprehension questions.
stages.content: Students read the text again and answer specific questions about Trang's leisure activities.

activities.action: Ask students to read the text again and answer the following questions.

Write answers to the questions: 1. What does Trang do with her family members at the weekend? 2. Who looks for recipes when Trang and her brother cook? 3. Which leisure activity does she love the most? 4. What does Trang's mum teach her to do? 5. What did she win?

stages.name: Stage 4: Group Discussion and Recording
stages.aim: To practice speaking skills by discussing personal leisure activities and recording peers' answers.
stages.content: Students work in groups to discuss leisure activities and record their peers' answers.

activities.action: Provide question sheets and explain the group discussion task.

Discuss in groups: 1. What leisure activities do you usually do with your family? 2. Which one do you like the most? Why? 3. How do you feel when you spend time with your family members? Record your friends' answers in the table.
[IMAGE_TAG_110]

stages.name: Stage 5: Reporting Group Findings
stages.aim: To report on group discussions and identify common leisure activities.
stages.content: Students report their group's findings to the class and discuss common leisure activities.

activities.action: Ask each group to report their findings to the class.
Present their group's answers and discuss which activities are the most common among their peers.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and consolidate learning.
stages.content: Teacher reviews key points and addresses any questions.

activities.action: Summarize the lesson's key vocabulary and grammar points.
Participate in the review by asking questions or sharing what they learned.

objectives: Develop listening comprehension skills by identifying key information from an audio interview about leisure activities.
Enhance speaking skills through pair discussions about personal leisure activities.
Practice writing skills by composing an email about leisure activities with friends.
Improve question formation using 'What,' 'Why,' and 'When'.
materials: Audio recording of the interview with Mark
Printed copies of the table for listening activity
Printed question sheets for pair discussions

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of leisure activities and engage students' interest.
stages.content: Teacher greets students and asks them about their typical leisure activities with friends.

activities.action: Ask students: 'What activities do you usually do with your friends in your leisure time?' and write their responses on the board.
Respond to the teacher's question with activities they do with friends.

stages.name: Stage 1: Pre-listening Discussion
stages.aim: To activate prior knowledge and set the context for listening.
stages.target_language: Leisure activities, spending time with friends
stages.content: Students discuss in pairs the questions about leisure activities with friends.

activities.action: Ask students to discuss in pairs: 1. In your opinion, what activities can we do with our friends in our leisure time? 2. Why should we spend time with our friends?
Discuss the questions in pairs and share their ideas with the class.

stages.name: Stage 2: Listening Comprehension - Multiple Choice
stages.aim: To listen to an interview and choose the correct answers to comprehension questions.
stages.content: Students listen to an interview with Mark and choose the correct answers.

activities.action: Play the audio recording of the interview with Mark.

Listen to the interview and choose the correct answer for each question: 1. When does Mark usually have free time? A. At weekends. B. On Sundays. C. On Saturdays. 2. Who does he spend his free time with? A. His neighbours and friends. B. His family and relatives. C. His family and friends.

stages.name: Stage 3: Listening Comprehension - Fill in the Blanks
stages.aim: To listen to the interview again and fill in the blanks with key information.
stages.target_language: Cooking, watching, going, fun, stay, see
stages.content: Students listen to the interview again and complete the table with no more than two words.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_128]
Play the audio recording again and instruct students to fill in the blanks in the table.

Listen and fill in the blanks: Activities - Cooking and watching a (1) ________, Going to the (3) ________ to play volleyball or skateboard, Going for a (5) ________. Reasons - Fun and better than going to the (2) ________, Stay in (4) ________, See different (6) ________.

stages.name: Stage 4: Pair Discussion
stages.aim: To practice speaking skills by discussing personal leisure activities with a partner.
stages.content: Students work in pairs to ask and answer questions about their own leisure activities.

activities.action: Provide question sheets and explain the pair discussion task.
Discuss in pairs: 1. When do you usually have free time? 2. What do you usually do with your friends in your free time? 3. Why do you do these activities?

stages.name: Stage 5: Writing an Email
stages.aim: To practice writing skills by composing an email about leisure activities.
stages.content: Students write an email to a penfriend about their leisure activities using their answers from the previous stage.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_135]
Explain the structure of an email and provide the starting and ending prompts.

Write an email (80-100 words) to a penfriend using the provided prompts: From: To: Subject: My free time Hi ________, It's nice to hear from you again. Let me tell you about ______________. Bye for now,

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and consolidate learning.
stages.content: Teacher reviews key points and addresses any questions.

activities.action: Summarize the lesson's key vocabulary and grammar points.
Participate in the review by asking questions or sharing what they learned.

objectives: Review and consolidate vocabulary related to leisure activities.
Practice forming complete sentences from given cues using appropriate verb forms.
Enhance understanding of gerunds following verbs of liking and disliking.
Improve grammar skills by filling in blanks with correct verb forms.

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of leisure activities and engage students' interest.
stages.content: Teacher greets students and asks them about their favorite leisure activities.

activities.action: Ask students: 'What are some leisure activities you enjoy?' and write their responses on the board.
Respond to the teacher's question with their favorite leisure activities.

stages.name: Stage 1: Vocabulary Completion
stages.aim: To complete sentences with appropriate leisure activities.
stages.target_language: Online, puzzles, DIY, messaging, fond of, keen on
stages.content: Students complete sentences using appropriate leisure activities.

activities.action: Explain the task and provide examples of leisure activities.

Complete the sentences: 1. Mai loves __________ online for about 30 minutes a day. She thinks puzzles are good for the brain. 2. My favourite leisure activity is __________. I can make many things myself, such as paper flowers and bracelets. 3. __________ is a popular way for teens to spend their free time. Many of them send messages to each other every day. 4. My brother is fond of __________ with his friends. On Sundays, he usually plays football, goes swimming, or plays badminton with them. 5. Tom spends one hour on the Internet almost every day. He is keen on __________.

stages.name: Stage 2: Sentence Construction
stages.aim: To write complete sentences from given cues using correct verb forms.
stages.target_language: Crazy about, interested, not fond, not into, keen on
stages.content: Students construct sentences from provided cues.

activities.action: Provide sentence cues and explain how to form complete sentences.

Write sentences: 1. my cousin / crazy about / play / computer / games. 2. they / interested / play / badminton / after school? 3. I / not fond / make models / because / I / not patient. 4. - why / you / not into / cook? - because / often / burn myself. 5. my friends / keen / do judo / and / they / go / judo club / every Sunday.

stages.name: Stage 3: Grammar Practice - Gerunds
stages.aim: To fill in blanks with the correct form of gerunds following verbs of liking and disliking.
stages.target_language: Enjoy cycling, don’t like reading, detests playing, love chatting, prefer doing
stages.content: Students fill in blanks with correct gerund forms of verbs.

activities.action: Explain the use of gerunds after verbs of liking and disliking.

Complete the sentences: 1. Does Tom enjoy (cycle) __________ in the park with his friends? 2. Some teenagers don’t like (read) __________ comic books. 3. Mai detests (play) __________ sport because it’s tiring. 4. Trang and Ann love (chat) __________ with each other in their free time. 5. What do Nam and Mark prefer (do) __________ at the weekend?

stages.name: Stage 4: Passage Completion
stages.aim: To complete a passage using the correct verb forms and additional words if necessary.
stages.target_language: Riding, reading, messaging, making, knitting, playing
stages.content: Students complete a passage about Ann using verbs in brackets.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_159]
Provide the passage and explain how to use the verbs in brackets to complete it.

Complete the passage: Ann is my best friend. She usually has free time at the weekend. She enjoys (1. ride) riding a horse at the riding club. Sometimes, she likes (2. read) ______ or (3. message) ______. She also loves (4. make) ______ and (5. knit) ______. However, there is one thing she doesn’t like doing in her free time. She dislikes (6. play) ______.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and consolidate learning.
stages.content: Teacher reviews key points and addresses any questions.

activities.action: Summarize the lesson's key vocabulary and grammar points.
Participate in the review by asking questions or sharing what they learned.

objectives: Conduct a survey on leisure activities among classmates.
Practice question formation and interviewing skills.
Collect and organize data in a structured format.
Report findings using structured responses based on collected data.
Use vocabulary related to leisure activities and expressions of likes and dislikes.
materials: Printed copies of the interview questions

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of leisure activities and engage students' interest.
stages.content: Teacher greets students and asks them about their typical leisure activities.

activities.action: Ask students: 'What are your favorite leisure activities to do with family and friends?' and write their responses on the board.
Respond to the teacher's question with their favorite leisure activities.

stages.name: Stage 1: Interview Preparation
stages.aim: To prepare students for conducting interviews by reviewing key questions and vocabulary.
stages.target_language: When, What, would, like doing, would like to try
stages.content: Students review the interview questions and practice asking them in pairs.

activities.action: Distribute printed copies of the interview questions and explain the task.

Practice asking and answering the interview questions in pairs: 1. When do you have free time? 2. What do you like doing with your family in your free time? 3. What do you like doing with your friends in your free time? 4. What leisure activities do you like doing on your own? 5. What leisure activities would you like to try in the future?

stages.name: Stage 2: Conducting Interviews
stages.aim: To conduct interviews with classmates and collect data on leisure activities.
stages.content: Students conduct interviews with classmates and record their answers in a table.

activities.action: Instruct students to form groups and conduct interviews with classmates from different groups.
Interview classmates using the prepared questions and record their answers in the provided table.
[IMAGE_TAG_172]

stages.name: Stage 3: Data Organization
stages.aim: To organize collected data in a structured format for reporting.
stages.content: Students organize their collected data in the provided table format.

activities.action: Guide students in organizing their collected data into the provided table.
Fill in the table with the collected answers: Student | Question 1 | Question 2 | Question 3 | Question 4 | Question 5.

stages.name: Stage 4: Reporting Findings
stages.aim: To report the findings of the survey to the class using guiding questions.
stages.content: Students report their findings to the class based on guiding questions.

activities.action: Provide guiding questions for reporting findings and explain the reporting process.

Prepare and present their group's findings to the class, answering the guiding questions: 1. How many students did you interview? 2. When do the students have free time? 3. What are the most popular activities they do with their family? 4. What are the most popular activities they do with their friends? 5. What are the most popular activities they do on their own? 6. What leisure activities would they like to try in the future?

stages.name: Stage 5: Pronunciation Practice
stages.aim: To practice correct pronunciation of the sounds /uː/ and /ʊ/ in words and sentences.
stages.content: Students practice pronouncing words with /uː/ and /ʊ/ sounds.

activities.action: Play an audio recording of words and sentences containing /uː/ and /ʊ/ sounds and ask students to repeat.
Listen and repeat the words and sentences, focusing on the correct pronunciation of /uː/ and /ʊ/ sounds.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and consolidate learning.
stages.target_language: Leisure activities, expressions of likes and dislikes
stages.content: Teacher reviews key points and addresses any questions.

activities.action: Summarize the lesson's key vocabulary and grammar points.
Participate in the review by asking questions or sharing what they learned.
